channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 is racing around the corner and down the finish line towards its release, with the countdown now on for the release date in May.

The sixth game in the Forza Horizon series, a spinoff from the Forza Motorsport franchise, Forza Horizon 6 has answered the cries from fans to bring the location of the game to Japan, with a sprawling map themed around the Asian nation.

If you can’t wait for the release date of Forza Horizon 6, Insider Gaming has the key details you need to know.

When is Forza Horizon 6 Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

Forza Horizon 6 is set for a full release on May 19, 2026, but early access means that some players will be able to dive in up to four days earlier.

The Premium Edition provides up to four days of early access to Forza Horizon 6, though it comes with a hefty price tag of $129.99. Meanwhile, the standard edition is free for those with an Xbox Game Pass subscription.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete Forza Horizon 6 Achievement List

While the early access may appeal to devoted fans of the franchise, the standard edition will suffice for the majority of players, and four days isn’t a vastly significant amount of time to be without the game.

In fact, players in some regions will actually get to play the game earlier than the official release date on May 19 due to time zone differences.

Forza Horizon 6 Start Times

Forza Horizon 6 has not publicly confirmed its release time, but there are some indications online of when players will be able to jump into the action.

As an online title, Forza Horizon 6 is expected to have a global release structure, dropping simultaneously for players around the world, rather than a staggered release, dropping at various times in different time zones.

  • More Insider Gaming: New Forza Horizon 6 Limited Edition Xbox Accessories Revealed

Additionally, the early access availability of Forza Horizon 6, for those who qualify, means two waves of an influx of players, and they’re expected to be separated by exactly four days.

The start times for various time zones across the world are shown in the table below, split into the release of early access and the full drop of the game. All times are courtesy of SteamDB.

Is Forza Horizon 6 going to be the best racing game we’ve seen in years? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Forza Horizon 6, check out the full reveal of the Japan map, and find out all of the PC specifications.

MOBILE APP

item.title: German Counter-Strike Player Banned for 10 Years after Slapping Opponent
item.content:encoded:
German Counter-Strike player and streamer MAUschine has been given a 10-year ban from competing by German tournament organizers Fragster and DACH after slapping an opponent on stage at a local German LAN.

What caused the slap at CAGGTUS LAN?

Following the conclusion of their Grand Final contest, the opposing teams were invited to the main stage to shake hands as part of the presentation.

In full view of the audience, and broadcast on the CAGGTUS Leipzig’s Twitch, MAUschine slapped Regnum4Games Fabian ‘Spidergum’ Salomon.

After a period of shared shock and bemusement, the player was asked to leave the stage by the host of the event.

The incident took place after a match in CAGGTUS Leipzig’s Counter-Strike tournament. CAGGTUS is a gaming festival that features a LAN hall, holding competitions in CS2, Valorant, League of Legends, Rocket League, and more.

The annual local LAN awards no prize money, with the winner of its main esports tournaments securing LAN tickets for next year’s event.

Therefore, the match that cost MAUschine 10 years of competition did not cost him anything financially; in fact, the player will have plenty of savings now that he is banned from the event and cannot buy a ticket to play for 10 years.

As for the motive behind the hit, the violent action seemed to have been sparked by trash talk during the match.

MAUschine receives 10-year ban from tournament organizers

Shouting back and forth between teams is extremely common at all levels of Counter-Strike, but especially normal at local LANs where teams are having fun and bantering between each other.

What is not normal is to physically assault a fellow player, something which both DACH and Fragster agree with, both making statements that banned the player for 10 years, referring the incident to ESIC.

DACH shared the following statement: “We do not tolerate physical assaults against other players at LAN and have acted accordingly.

MAUschine is banned for at least 10 years, and the incident has additionally been reported to ESIC. In case it wasn’t clear: We think violence is pretty sh*tty and it has no place in our league.” (translated)

Both bans are somewhat preliminary in nature, as they are subject to ESIC’s decision. Following an investigation from ESIC, the Tournament Organizers will uphold any ban that is decided by the body.

ESIC, the Esports Integrity Commission, is a not-for-profit organization that governs integrity issues for partnered tournaments. While the body has been criticized in the past for slow action, they have clear and recorded evidence to work with to adjudge the punishment for MAUschine’s slap.

For more Counter-Strike discussion, why not join Insider Gaming’s Discord.
For more CS news, see how Vitality captured their historic second Grand Slam.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Invincible VS Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
April is coming to an end, and what better way to bookend the month than with Invincible VS, the new 3v3 tag fighting game set in the brutal and gory Invincible universe.

With 18 characters forming the roster at launch, you’ll be able to settle the scores once and for all between some of your favourite characters. Ever wonder how Rex Splode would cope against Conquest? Now’s the time to find out, as Invincible VS is almost here.

For all the release date details for Invincible VS, as well as exact start times, Insider Gaming has got you covered.

When Is Invincible VS Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

Stand ready for its arrival, worm, as Invincible VS is coming in hot, with a release date of April 30, 2026, set in stone.

After a successful open beta in early April, it’s full steam ahead for Invincible VS’ official launch. Whilst the gameplay had to be right, there were never any questions about a fighting game set in the Invincible universe, as it seemed like the perfect setting to beat characters to a pulp—after all, it happens every week in the show!

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced Releases July 9, 2026

Thankfully, if you’re a fan of the animated show and comics, it looks like the game is emulating the gore and violence perfectly. With brutal finishers and blockbuster action, you can soon host your dream superpowered match-ups.

Invincible VS Start Times

Make no mistake about it, as Invincible VS will be ready and playable on April 30, 2026, at 10 AM PT.

It’s perfect news for those on the West Coast, as you’ll have practically the whole day to get to grips with the enthralling 3v3 tag gameplay.

  • More Insider Gaming: Metro 2039 Release Window Officially Confirmed

However, there’s bound to be Invincible fans from across the world hoping to dive in on day one, so with that in mind, here’s when you can play Invincible VS, adjusted for numerous time zones:

Are you excited to play Invincible VS? Who will be your main? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming release hubs, check out the following:

  • NTE: Neverness To Everness
  • Saros
  • ARC Raiders Riven Tides

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Is NTE: Neverness to Everness on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Neverness to Everness is set to make waves with its upcoming release, with the title expected to be hugely popular, but the available platforms present some restrictions.

The free-to-play title is set to offer plenty to experience, with GTA Online-style features such as property ownership, a variety of jobs, and online races.

If you’re on the hunt to learn which platforms Neverness to Everness will be available on, Insider Gaming has you covered.

Is Neverness to Everness on PlayStation?

Yes, Neverness to Everness is available to play on PlayStation when it launches on April 29, but only on current-gen consoles.

That means that while players on PS5 will be able to dive into the game, those on a PlayStation 4 will go without. Additionally, it’s unlikely the game will ever come to PS4.

While the prices for a PS5 are rising, making it more expensive to upgrade to the latest gen, it shouldn’t be much of a surprise to see PS4 players miss out, as it has been years since the PS5 was released.

  • More Insider Gaming: NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Is Neverness to Everness on Xbox?

No, Neverness to Everness is not available to play on Xbox platforms upon release on April 29, including the latest gen.

Both Xbox Series X|S players and Xbox One players miss out on the title, though there is a chance the game could feature on the latest generation of consoles later down the line.

Many Asian-developed titles are not initially developed on Xbox, with PlayStation being the more popular console in the region, but it doesn’t rule out a move to the platform in future.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Easily Complete All ARC Raiders Trials This Week (April 20)

Is Neverness to Everness on PC?

Yes, Neverness to Everness will be available to play on PC on the April 29 launch date, although storefronts like Steam and the Epic Games Store won’t have the title at launch.

Instead, Neverness to Everness has its own separate launcher that must be downloaded to play the games. However, this shouldn’t present too much of an issue, and is fairly standard for these types of games.

In future, Neverness to Everness could be accessible through storefronts like Steam, but initially, the free-to-play title has some limitations.

What platform are you planning to play Neverness to Everness on? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at every video game releasing in April 2026, and find out how to easily complete all ARC Raiders trials this week.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
New titles continue to be released thick and fast in 2026, and while there are some hefty and potentially expensive games on the horizon, the imminent Neverness to Everness release date provides plenty of free-to-play entertainment.

The Gacha title is set to make significant waves upon release, having attracted a huge amount of attention with pre-registrations, and the release date is now rapidly approaching.

For all the release date details for Neverness to Everness, including exact start times, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When is Neverness to Everness Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

Neverness to Everness is locked in for release on April 29, 2026, and the title is surging headfirst into its release, with no delays set to take place.

The release date for Neverness to Everness was shared earlier this year, and the new gacha game is ready to make waves after attracting a host of attention during development.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Saros Releasing On PC? All Saros Platforms, Explained

Neverness to Everness has achieved the remarkable achievement of receiving over 35 million pre-registration awards, alongside over five million social media followers, displaying the vast excitement there is for the title’s release.

With GTA Online-style life-sim elements, including property ownership, part-time jobs, street races, and more, there’s plenty to enjoy in NTE.

Neverness to Everness Start Times

No official start time has been shared so far for Neverness to Everness, though there are some hints at exactly when the title will be available.

While the release time has not been shared, it has been revealed that pre-downloading will begin on April 27 at 10 PM ET, which, if the times remain the same for the full launch, means that players in some time zones should be able to start playing before the official release date of April 29.

  • More Insider Gaming: Can You Play Fortnite Save The World on Mobile?

As Neverness to Everness is an online game, the release will be global, releasing simultaneously to players across the world, and won’t have a staggered release time.

The table below shows expected start times for Neverness to Everness based on the pre-download time. This article will be updated with official details when available.

Are you excited for the release of Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts on the title in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at every video game releasing in April 2026, and find out how to easily complete all ARC Raiders trials this week.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vitality’s Dominance Shows No Signs of Stopping Following Second Grand Slam at IEM Rio 2026
item.content:encoded:
Team Vitality’s roster has further cemented its place in Counter-Strike history after their IEM Rio 2026 victory secured the team’s second ESL Grand Slam.

Vitality makes history with Grand Slam victory

The roster became the first team to secure two Grand Slams with a 3-0 Grand Final victory over Team Spirit. The question was not if Vitality would win the Grand Slam again, but when, and they did so on the first time of asking at IEM Rio.

The roster will now begin their chase for an unprecedented third Grand Slam at IEM Atlanta in May. With four ESL events remaining in 2026, Vitality can theoretically win another Grand Slam this year, provided there are no rule changes to the Grand Slam requirements.

The most important of those tournaments will be the IEM Cologne 2026 Major, where Vitality will hope to equal Astralis’ record of three Majors in a row, and four in total for the organization.

Intel/ESL Grand Slam winners:

  • Vitality – 2: Season VI, Season 5
  • FaZe – 1: Season 4
  • NAVI – 1: Season 3
  • Liquid – 1: Season 2
  • Astralis – 1: Season 1

Robin ‘ropz’ Kool became the first player to secure three Grand Slams following the victory, combining his two gold bars from Vitality with FaZe’s Grand Slam, which they secured in 2023.

The chasing pack feels no closer to Vitality

Coming into the event with a shaky win against G2, Vitality looked more vulnerable at IEM Rio as they chased the Grand Slam.

That feeling was cemented by Vitality’s first defeat since January, as they lost 2-1 to Falcons in the Upper Final, breaking their 18-series win streak.

That feeling of vulnerability completely dissipated once Vitality entered the server on the stage, blowing NAVI away, before breaking Brazilian hearts in the Semi-Final.

Spirit were much improved in this tournament, but they still could not reach the heights of Vitality’s eminence.

While Vitality still did not look their commanding best at the IEM Rio Grand Final, their floor was still unreachable to a Spirit roster who suffered from their star, Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets, not showing his usual individual level.

It continues a pattern of Vitality dashing the hopes of improving rosters, as NAVI felt that same pain at IEM Rio and BLAST Rotterdam.

Andrii ‘B1ad3’ Horodenskyi joked to the ESL broadcast that NAVI should simply avoid events Vitality attends, but they are unfortunately locked in with Vitality for the American swing of the calendar, both attending BLAST Fort Worth, then IEM Atlanta.

Spirit, on the other hand, will be looking forward to time away from Counter-Strike’s most dominant roster, attending PGL Astana instead of Atlanta from May 9-17.

Those events will be proof enough of whether their IEM Rio growth was concrete or ephemeral, as the Major looms large.

For Falcons, the event felt their most positive event since BLAST Bounty Season 1, which they played with a stand-in, but it ended in an equally brutal way with Spirit dismantling the team.

It is likely the last time we see this roster, with rumours of Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen’s arrival all but confirmed. They will also avoid Vitality at Astana and the CS Asia Championships, a potentially perfect honeymoon period for the Falcons roster.

Are Vitality the greatest Counter-Strike team of all time? Discuss that on our Discord!
For more news, see FalleN’s retirement announcement at IEM Rio 2026.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
